skill.name: hermesemailskill
skill.description: Plan, review, audit, and improve email launch work with clear QA checks, decision summaries, and approval gates.
skill.summary: Coordinate email launch readiness with concise QA, risk, and handoff guidance.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: The four high-severity static findings were evaluated as false positives. The cited lines contain ordinary words such as description, describe, or destructive, with no cryptographic code or weak algorithm use. No prompt injection, exfiltration intent, command execution, network access, or filesystem risk was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is frontmatter description text for email workflow triggers. No hash, cipher, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains descriptive metadata, not executable code. The apparent match is a substring in the word description.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged agents/openai.yaml line 2, but the line is a short marketplace description. No cryptographic primitive or algorithm selection is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a natural language short_description field. There is no code path that could invoke weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged references/operating-checklist.md line 18, but the line asks reviewers to compare a CTA and subject line. It does not mention or use cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious token is part of ordinary checklist prose. The surrounding context is email QA guidance, not security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged references/operating-checklist.md line 27, but the line lists high-risk email operations. The word destructive likely triggered the match.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a risk gate that requires approval before live changes. It reduces operational risk instead of introducing weak cryptography.

content.value_statement: Email launches can fail when QA, approvals, and incident status are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through concise checks, summaries, and handoffs.
actual_capabilities: Creates preflight checklists for copy, links, rendering, segments, sender identity, and tracking.
Summarizes launch status as ready, ready with caveats, blocked, or needs review.
Drafts incident timelines with cause, impact, mitigation, and next owner.
Reviews transactional and lifecycle email copy against the stated user promise.
Separates low-risk analysis from live actions that need explicit approval.
limitations: It does not send emails or change provider settings by itself.
It depends on the launch brief, previews, test sends, and provider details supplied by the user.
It cannot guarantee deliverability or inbox placement.
It does not replace legal, compliance, or final business approval.

use_cases.title: Preflight a Campaign Launch
use_cases.description: Build a concise readiness check before a scheduled send.

use_cases.title: Review Transactional Copy
use_cases.description: Check message clarity, user promise, fallback values, and required footer elements.

use_cases.title: Coordinate an Email Incident
use_cases.description: Create a timeline, impact summary, mitigation plan, and owner handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Launch Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Hermes Email Skill to review this email launch brief. Return readiness status, blockers, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Transactional Message Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Hermes Email Skill to audit this transactional email. Check user promise, links, merge tags, fallback values, and approval needs.

prompt_templates.title: Launch Room Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Hermes Email Skill to turn these launch notes into a concise status handoff with owners, risks, deadlines, and approval state.

prompt_templates.title: Incident Triage Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Hermes Email Skill to triage this email incident. Build a timeline, blast radius, suspected cause, mitigation, monitoring plan, and next owner.

output_examples.input: Review a promotional send scheduled today with final copy, segment notes, and test-send feedback.
output: Next action: Marketing owns exclusion validation by 15:00 UTC.

output_examples.input: Summarize an incident where password reset emails were delayed for one hour.
output: Impact: Password reset delivery was delayed for affected users.
Mitigation: Provider queue cleared and monitoring remains active.
Owner: Engineering confirms recovery and Support prepares user messaging.

output_examples.input: Review a transactional receipt email before launch.
output: Copy risk: The subject and call to action do not match the receipt promise.
QA need: Test fallback values for missing order fields.
Approval gate: Do not launch until compliance confirms footer requirements.

best_practices: Provide the launch deadline, audience, provider, approvals, and latest test-send results.
Separate blockers from polish issues so owners can act quickly.
Require explicit approval before live sends, contact imports, DNS changes, or suppression edits.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the agent to approve or send production email without human confirmation.
Do not skip segment exclusions or suppression checks when time is short.
Do not treat deliverability concerns as copy-only issues.

faq.question: Can this skill send emails?
faq.answer: No. It provides review, planning, and handoff guidance. Live sends need explicit human approval and a separate tool.

faq.question: What inputs work best?
faq.answer: Provide the brief, audience, deadline, copy, preview, test-send notes, provider status, and approval owner.

faq.question: Does it cover transactional emails?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks user promise, clarity, fallback values, links, footer requirements, and launch risks.

faq.question: Can it help during incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize timelines, suspected cause, impact, mitigation, monitoring, and owner handoffs.

faq.question: Does it replace compliance review?
faq.answer: No. It can flag compliance concerns, but legal and compliance owners must make final decisions.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
